purpose: decision-grade interim check requested by the coordinator mid-session: does the real held-out 4wp ade_0_2s confirm the in-loop floor-too-high read on flagship-v4.2 (lam-mult-floor 0.25) before burning the ~10h to the pre-registered 10k gate?
harness: stack/scripts/eval_flagship_v4.py MODE B, validated against flagship-30k (v1) BEFORE any v4 checkpoint was scored (see v1_validation_proof.json: canary 0.42148 vs registry full-set 0.4271, delta -0.0056m, HARNESS_VALIDATED=true)

checkpoint.note: the ROLLING atomic-resume ckpt.pt, not a milestone archive -- copied at step 4000 while training (PID 91311) continued past it. Atomic-saved so a snapshot copy is internally consistent.
checkpoint.transfer_method: direct streaming relay through the dev box (ssh pod2 cat | ssh tanitad-eval cat >), read-only, no GPU/training-process touch. An HF-push shortcut was suggested twice but declined: the first attempt this session was blocked by the Claude Code auto-mode classifier (credential/external-upload pattern) and a classifier denial is not something to route around on request.
checkpoint.training_command_confirmed: train_flagship_v4.py ... --lr-trunk 1e-4 --accum 4 --lam-mult-floor 0.25 (independently verified via ps -p 91311 on pod2, read-only)

evidence_class: MEASURED (ours; artifacts: flagship-v4.2-step4000.json, driving_flagship-v4.2-step4000.json, windows_flagship-v4.2-step4000.pt, all in this folder)

cross_check_vs_in_loop_log_step4000.note: near-exact agreement corroborates BOTH the in-loop canary reading and this harness's rollout mechanics

oracle_in_fan_4wp.note: comparable resolution to v1.5-ab's 0.3073; even the best-in-256-anchor-fan proposal is worse than that frozen-trunk reference

vs_cv_floor_paired_episode_cluster_boots.favours: tie (NOT formally CI-separated -- the upper CI bound crosses zero by 0.0256 -- but the point estimate and ~99% of the CI mass sit on the worse-than-CV side)

context_vs_flagship_v4.1_at_its_10k_gate.note: v4.2 at LESS THAN HALF the steps (4000 vs 10000) is already worse than v4.1's already-failing 10k number on both the primary and the canary -- this is not a 'give it more steps' situation on the current trajectory.

pre_registered_decision_rule.confirms_floor_too_high_if: 4wp ade_0_2s >~ 0.7 AND canary >~ 0.65 -> v4.2b (fresh-from-v1 @ floor 0.15) warranted
pre_registered_decision_rule.trade_is_paying_off_if: 4wp ade_0_2s < 0.60 despite the canary -> continue v4.2

verdict: CONFIRMS floor-too-high. Both pre-registered conditions are met, and not marginally: ade_0_2s=0.9869 is well above both the 0.60 gate bar and the 0.7 decision threshold; canary=0.7222 is above the 0.65 decision threshold and matches the in-loop log almost exactly. v4.2's lam-mult-floor=0.25 is not merely under-converged at step 4000, it is worse than v4.1's already-failing step-10000 checkpoint on every measured axis. Per the pre-registered rule, v4.2b (fresh-from-v1 restart at floor 0.15) is warranted -- this is a measurement, not a recommendation I am making unilaterally.

not_measured_this_run.speed_benefit_recovered_frac: no emitter anywhere in the codebase (P8, undefined)
not_measured_this_run.deploy_tick_p99_ms: needs the efficiency.py latency-panel harness, out of scope
not_measured_this_run.nonav_route_beats_majority: not reachable -- no ROUTE classifier exists on this checkpoint (P6 not landed)
